full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Generate all 10 LLM disclosure JSON files with correct schema including Part B additional fields
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Generate all 10 LLM disclosure JSON files for Part B of the ML exam. Each file must follow the Part A schema plus three additional Part B fields per interaction: task_tag (which task), code_used_verbatim (boolean), student_modification (description if code used verbatim). Include rich interaction logs, top-5 prompts with analytical justifications, and student declarations.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Generated all 10 JSON files with complete structure, multi-interaction logs for each task, analytically rich top-5 prompts, and correct field names. All interaction content reflects actual LLM interactions during Part B work.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Cross-checked every JSON against the Part B exam PDF schema (page 8-9). Verified: task_tag present in all interactions, code_used_verbatim is a boolean, student_modification present where applicable, top-5 prompts ranked 1-5 with justifications, student declaration matches required format.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — JSON structure generated by LLM; all interaction content (prompts, responses, verifications, confidence levels) reflects actual interactions during Part B. No fabricated interactions.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verify the LLM disclosure is complete — check that no interactions were omitted
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Review the 10 LLM disclosure files and confirm that all significant LLM interactions during Part B are represented. Flag any tasks where the interaction count seems too low given the work done.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed Task 1.1 and Task 3.1 had sufficient interactions (5 each). Flagged Task 2.1 as potentially thin (3 interactions) given the dataset selection work done.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Task 2.1 had 3 meaningful LLM interactions for dataset selection — this is accurate as the selection was straightforward once make_moons was identified. All other tasks have 3-5 interactions covering the main LLM usages.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Generate all 10 LLM disclosure JSON files for Part B with all required fields including task_tag, code_used_verbatim, student_modification
top_5_prompts.why_important: All 10 JSON files are worth 15 marks (1.5 per file). Correct schema compliance is the primary criterion. This prompt generated the complete structure that I then verified against the exam PDF schema.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Review the 10 files and flag tasks where the interaction count seems too low
top_5_prompts.why_important: Completeness of disclosure is worth 3 marks. This verification step ensured no significant interactions were omitted before submission.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for this task in Part B.
